# GoodAgent — GoodDollar Agent ID (guide for AI agents)

> Passport-free Proof-of-Human for AI agents on Celo. A face-verified GoodDollar
> human signs an EIP-712 credential vouching for an agent address and locks a
> refundable G$ bond (>= 250 G$) behind it. Anyone can verify, live, that a real
> human still stands behind the agent.

TL;DR — the three things you can do:
1. Get registered (once): FIRST you attest on-chain that you control your
   address (required — unattested addresses are rejected), THEN a verified
   human stakes a 250 G$ refundable bond behind it and signs your credential.
2. Check anyone's status (no auth): GET /agent/verify/<address> — live verdict.
3. Authenticate a counterparty: a credential proves an ADDRESS is vouched for,
   not that the party presenting it controls that address. When trust or money
   is on the line, require a fresh agent-signed challenge via
   POST /agent/verify-auth.

Base URL: https://goodagentids.xyz/api
Contract addresses and endpoints: see section 5 (Reference).

## 1. Get registered

Registration is a two-sided handshake: FIRST the agent proves it controls its
address, THEN a human operator vouches for it. The API rejects registrations
for unattested addresses with AGENT_NOT_ATTESTED.

Step 1 — YOU attest your key (required, do this first). Attest once in the
AgentAttestation registry (0xe5EFd6755e8a2035c924f9BaCDecD067B3dcf6C2, Celo).
The fact is stored permanently so anyone can verify it without trusting the
API; verify responses carry agentProven: true (+ agentProvenAt). Two ways:

A. You hold gas (CELO): call attest() from your own account — msg.sender IS
   the proof. ABI: function attest() external.

B. You hold no gas: sign an EIP-712 AttestAgent message and let anyone relay
   it via attestFor(agent, deadline, signature). Single-use (bound to your
   on-chain nonce) and deadline-bound.
   Domain: { name: "GoodDollar Agent Attestation", version: "1",
             chainId: 42220, verifyingContract: <registry> }
   Types:  AttestAgent { agent address, nonce uint256, deadline uint256 }
   SDK: attestationTypedData + getAttestationNonce (@goodagent/agent-id).

Off-chain alternative (accepted instead of the on-chain attestation): include
a signed AgentAuth challenge (audience "gooddollar-agent-id:register", struct
in section 3) as "agentProof" in the POST /agent/issue body. The on-chain
route is preferred: it is publicly verifiable forever.

Steps 2-5 — your human operator vouches for you, everything signed in THEIR
wallet (you never hold their keys):

2. Your operator gets GoodDollar face verification (free, no passport) at
   https://wallet.gooddollar.org and holds >= 250 G$ on Celo.
3. They open https://goodagentids.xyz/issue and enter your agent
   address (the address you act/sign with).
4. They approve and stake a refundable bond of >= 250 G$ into the AgentVault
   contract behind your address. The bond is a deposit, never a fee: it only
   ever returns to the operator (withdrawal has a 3-day cooldown).
5. They sign the EIP-712 AgentID credential in their own wallet (non-custodial,
   free) and it is stored via POST /agent/issue (the API re-verifies the
   attestation, the signature, the live whitelist, and the bond before
   storing).

How you'll know it happened — poll the public verify endpoint until your
address turns up valid:

    GET https://goodagentids.xyz/api/agent/verify/<your-address>
    -> repeat every ~30s until { "found": true, "valid": true }

Trustless alternatives:
- Watch the AgentVault's Staked(agent, operator, amount) event for your address
  on Celo. The bond lands a moment before the credential is issued, so confirm
  via the API afterwards.
- If your operator anchored the credential in your ERC-8004 registration, read
  your own registry metadata under the key "gooddollar-proof-of-human".

Note: the verify endpoint proves you are vouched for but does not return the
signed credential itself. If you want to carry and present the credential
(offline verification, ERC-8004 embedding), ask your operator for the
credential JSON after they sign it.

## 2. Verify any agent (identity lookup)

No auth required:

    GET https://goodagentids.xyz/api/agent/verify/<agent-address>

Registered and backed:

    { "found": true, "valid": true, "operator": "0x...", "humanRoot": "0x...",
      "expiresAt": "<unix-seconds>", "bondChecked": true,
      "revocationChecked": true, "agentProven": true,
      "onchain": { "stake": "...", "minStake": "..." } }

`agentProven` reports the agent's key proof-of-possession (on-chain
attestation, or an agentProof accepted at issue time). New registrations are
proven by construction — the API rejects unattested addresses. Registrations
that predate this rule may still show agentProven: false.

Not registered: { "found": false, "valid": false, "reason": "not_found" }

`valid` is computed live on every call: EIP-712 signature, expiry, the
operator's current GoodDollar verification, the on-chain revocation registry,
and the on-chain G$ bond. Failure reasons: expired, revoked (operator flipped
the on-chain kill switch or registry flag), operator_not_verified,
human_root_mismatch, insufficient_bond (the bond was withdrawn below the
250 G$ minimum). bondChecked / revocationChecked tell you whether the live
reads actually ran.

Optional: `?minStake=<base-units>` reports whether the live bond also meets a
verifier-chosen higher minimum (`meetsMinStake`).

Same check, three other surfaces:

TypeScript SDK (viem-only): npm install @goodagent/agent-id

    import { verifyAgentIdLive } from "@goodagent/agent-id";
    // all live checks on by default: human root, revocation, bond
    const { valid, reason, bondChecked, revocationChecked } =
      await verifyAgentIdLive(credential);

MCP: npx -y @goodagent/mcp-server (stdio). Tool: gooddollar_verify_agent
(pass a full credential object; valid only if human-backed AND not revoked
AND bonded). Other tools: gooddollar_verify_status, gooddollar_get_balance,
gooddollar_claim_eligibility, gooddollar_get_daily_stats, gooddollar_ping.

List an operator's agents: GET /agent/list?operator=0x... (or ?humanRoot=0x...)
Browser Explorer: https://goodagentids.xyz/verify

IMPORTANT — this is an identity LOOKUP, not authentication. It answers "is
address X human-backed?", not "is the party I'm talking to really X?".
Credentials are public; anyone can present one. When it matters, use section 3.

## 3. Authenticate a counterparty (proof-of-possession)

Ask the agent to sign a fresh EIP-712 AgentAuth challenge with its own key,
then submit it — one call checks both the signature and the credential:

EIP-712 domain: { name: "GoodDollar Agent ID", version: "1", chainId: 42220 }
EIP-712 types:  AgentAuth { agent address, audience string,
                            nonce uint256, issuedAt uint64 }

    POST https://goodagentids.xyz/api/agent/verify-auth
    { "auth": { "agent": "0x...", "audience": "<your-service>",
                "nonce": "<n>", "issuedAt": "<unix-seconds>",
                "signature": "0x..." },
      "audience": "<your-service>" }

Returns { "authenticated": true, "valid": true, ... } only if the signature
recovers to the agent address (fresh, max age 2 min) AND the credential behind
it verifies live.

- audience is REQUIRED and scopes the proof to one service so it can't be
  replayed elsewhere. It must match what the agent signed.
- issuedAt gives it a 2-minute freshness window.
- nonce is single-use: the registry rejects a replayed auth even inside the
  window. Use a random nonce for every auth (the SDK does this by default).

SDK equivalents: buildAgentAuth + signAgentAuth (agent side),
verifyAgentAuth + verifyAgentIdLive (verifier side).

## 4. The rules you live under

- Live, not snapshot: every verification re-reads the GoodDollar whitelist,
  the AgentRevocation registry, and the AgentVault bond on-chain. Validity
  ends the moment any of them lapses.
- The bond must stay locked: withdrawing it below 250 G$ un-vouches you
  (insufficient_bond) until re-staked. Withdrawing is how an operator
  un-vouches you.
- Your operator can revoke you at any time via the on-chain AgentRevocation
  kill switch (seen by every verifier, reversible).
- One human can vouch for at most 10 active agents.
- The credential is identity-only: it grants no spending power and carries no
  money fields. Credentials expire (default up to 1 year) and can be revoked.
- The credential is NOT a bearer token. Never treat a presented credential as
  proof of identity without a fresh AgentAuth signature from the agent's key.

## 5. Reference

Endpoints (base: https://goodagentids.xyz/api):
- GET  /agent/verify/<address>?minStake=   identity lookup (live verdict)
- POST /agent/verify-auth                  authenticated verify (possession)
- POST /agent/issue                        store an operator-signed credential
- GET  /agent/list?operator=0x...          list agents (or ?humanRoot=0x...)
- GET  /wallet/<address>                   GoodDollar status of any wallet
- GET  /health

Contracts — Celo mainnet (chainId 42220):
- AgentVault (required refundable G$ bond): 0x0409042B55e99Df8c0Feb7525A770838f3A47090
- AgentRevocation (on-chain operator kill switch): 0xA86a133626989115a6499b6cA67c3c8dA1662137
- AgentAttestation (on-chain key proof-of-possession): 0xe5EFd6755e8a2035c924f9BaCDecD067B3dcf6C2
- GoodDollarHumanProofProvider (ERC-8004 IHumanProofProvider): 0x80c4de6872049cb20989156bca50134c781f48c9
- G$ token: 0x62B8B11039FcfE5aB0C56E502b1C372A3d2a9c7A
- GoodDollar Identity: 0xC361A6E67822a0EDc17D899227dd9FC50BD62F42
- ERC-8004 Identity Registry: 0x8004A169FB4a3325136EB29fA0ceB6D2e539a432

Apps & packages:
- Web app / Explorer: https://goodagentids.xyz
- Quickstart (human-readable): https://goodagentids.xyz/quickstart.md
- Agent guide: https://goodagentids.xyz/for-agents
- Demo agent (attested, canonical walkthrough): 0xBd4495328ac79B2E4A4B488Eb0D4b3548833Ad2A
- npm SDK: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@goodagent/agent-id
- npm MCP server: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@goodagent/mcp-server
